description:
Sixth subticket. Depends on .18.5.

Add @doc and @moduledoc syntax. Inert metadata in v1 — stored on the AST so the test runner (.16) can use @doc/@moduledoc strings as descriptions, and a future doc system can render them.

## Scope

- Lexer: `@` token (Tok::At). Bare `@name` followed by an expression at top of a module item / module body.
- Parser:
  - Inside a defmodule, `@moduledoc "..."` attaches a doc string to the module.
  - Above a fn/defmacro, `@doc "..."` attaches a doc string to that item.
- AST:
  - ModuleDef gains `moduledoc: Option<String>`.
  - FnDef gains `doc: Option<String>`.
- Eval/codegen: ignore — metadata only.

## Out of scope

- Other attributes: @spec, @type, @callback, @impl. File followups if needed.
- Custom @-attributes (Module.register_attribute style).

## Tests

- Parse a defmodule with @moduledoc and a fn with @doc; assert the strings land on the AST.
- Existing programs without attributes still parse/run unchanged.
title: Module attributes (@doc, @moduledoc)
